utility_evaluation.REASONING: The required format lists 5 items: health_coverage, property_coverage, travel_coverage, additional_services, total_budget. The final plan includes all 5 corresponding sections. Now check if each is fully specified with all required details: Each of health_coverage, property_coverage, and travel_coverage includes a description aligning to the required coverage type and provides explicit monthly and annual costs. Additional_services is included with a service and details, but it lacks an explicit cost amount (it says cost included, but no numeric cost), yet the format only requires 'any enhancement services with costs'—the absence of a numeric cost could be considered incomplete. However, since it states cost included in health plan setup, the cost is accounted for indirectly; still, the item asks for costs, so this is arguably under-specified. Total_budget provides total monthly and annual costs as required. Therefore: 4 items are fully specified (health, property, travel, total_budget). Additional_services is present but not fully specified with a numeric cost.

llm_ratings_evaluation.REASONING: The final coverage plan includes three named insurance plans: LifeFirst Standard Plan (health), LifeFirst Standard Coverage (property/home contents), and InsurePlus Standard Plan (travel). We must match each to the corresponding ground-truth rating. From the ground-truth: Primary health coverage -> physical_health -> LifeFirst Standard Plan has rating 9. Property protection -> home_content -> LifeFirst Standard Coverage has rating 9. Mobility coverage -> travel_insurance -> InsurePlus Standard Plan has rating 10.
